name: Group 1577
denotation: a power button symbol (broken circle with a vertical line through the top)
visual_elements: vertical line segment piercing the top gap of the circle
best_for: enabling or disabling a feature or account
avoid_for: anything unrelated to binary on/off state, since the glyph reads specifically as a power symbol
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a padlock shackle icon at small sizes due to the circle-plus-vertical-line shape

name: H1
denotation: the bold capital letters 'H1'
avoid_for: contexts unrelated to text formatting, since the glyph is literally letterform-based and reads only as a heading-level label
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'H2'/'H3' at a glance if the digit isn't legible at very small sizes

name: H2
denotation: the bold capital letters 'H2'
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'H1'/'H3' at a glance if the digit isn't legible at very small sizes

name: H3
denotation: the bold capital letters 'H3'
connotations: deepest common sub-hierarchy in a document outline
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'H1'/'H2' at a glance if the digit isn't legible at very small sizes

name: ai
denotation: a cluster of three four-pointed sparkle/star shapes of varying sizes
connotations: an AI-powered enhancement applied to something
avoid_for: decorative/celebratory contexts unrelated to AI, since sparkles alone can read as generic 'new' or 'special' badges rather than specifically AI
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'new'/'featured' badge or a cleaning/sparkle effect rather than specifically artificial intelligence without surrounding context

name: glossary-ai
denotation: an open book with a sparkle icon at its top-right corner
visual_elements: open book with visible center spine
best_for: AI-suggested or AI-generated glossary term definitions
smart documentation features tied to a knowledge base
avoid_for: a plain, non-AI glossary or reading feature, since the sparkle specifically signals an automation/AI layer
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'help/read the docs' icon if the sparkle is missed at very small sizes

name: language-ai
denotation: a foreign-language character next to the letter A, flanked by two small sparkles
avoid_for: generic text-editing actions with no translation or language component
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'text/typography' icon if the foreign-language glyph isn't recognized as a translation symbol

name: link-obj-ai
denotation: two circular nodes joined by a curved connecting line, with a sparkle beside them
connotations: AI-suggested relationship or link between two objects
best_for: auto-mapping or auto-linking features in a data catalog
avoid_for: a plain manual link/connect action with no automated or AI component
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic process-flow or path icon if the sparkle is overlooked

name: redo-ai
denotation: a circular arrow (redo/refresh loop) flanked by two sparkles
visual_elements: arrowhead at one end forming a loop
best_for: a 'regenerate with AI' button on generated content
avoid_for: a plain, non-AI redo/refresh action where the sparkle would misleadingly imply an AI feature
possible_misreadings: could be read as a plain 'refresh/reload' icon if the sparkles are too small to notice

name: text-ai
denotation: a pencil drawn diagonally, flanked by two sparkles
visual_elements: two four-pointed sparkles at opposite corners
best_for: 'improve with AI' or 'rewrite' actions on text fields
avoid_for: a plain manual edit action, since the sparkles specifically signal an AI-augmented capability
possible_misreadings: could be read as a plain 'edit' pencil icon if the sparkles are missed at small render sizes

name: arrow-down-right
denotation: a diagonal arrow pointing to the lower right
visual_elements: arrowhead bent at the bottom-right end
connotations: movement or growth toward a lower-right destination
decline when paired with a downward trend context
best_for: indicating a downward-and-rightward trend or flow
avoid_for: strictly vertical or horizontal movement, where a cardinal-direction arrow would be clearer
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'expand/resize' diagonal arrows if shown alone without a paired opposite arrow

name: arrow-down
denotation: a single straight arrow pointing downward
connotations: moving to a lower position or later step
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'download' icon if paired with a tray shape, though alone it is a pure directional arrow

name: arrow-drop-down
denotation: a small chevron pointing downward
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open at the top
avoid_for: a full directional-movement arrow context (e.g. trend charts), where the plain arrow-down icon is more literal
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'collapse' or the plain down-arrow at very small sizes since the chevron shapes are visually similar across this set

name: arrow-drop-right
denotation: a small chevron pointing to the right
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open toward the left, tip pointing right
connotations: moving forward or deeper into a hierarchy
avoid_for: a definitive 'forward navigation between pages' context, where a full arrow-right reads more decisively
possible_misreadings: could be confused with carret-right in this same set since both are right-pointing chevrons of similar weight

name: arrow-drop-up
denotation: a small chevron pointing upward
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open at the bottom, tip pointing up
connotations: increase or improvement when used in trend contexts
best_for: collapsing an expanded dropdown or panel
avoid_for: contexts needing a strong/large directional statement, where the full arrow-up icon reads more clearly
possible_misreadings: could be confused with the full arrow-up icon or with carret shapes at a glance

name: arrow-left
denotation: a single straight arrow pointing left
visual_elements: triangular arrowhead at the left end
best_for: moving items left in a reorderable list
possible_misreadings: minimal risk — a plain leftward arrow reads unambiguously as 'back' or 'left' in most UI conventions

name: arrow-right
denotation: a single straight arrow pointing right
visual_elements: triangular arrowhead at the right end
best_for: moving items right in a list
possible_misreadings: minimal risk — a plain rightward arrow reads unambiguously as 'forward' or 'next'

name: arrow-up-left
denotation: a bent arrow pointing to the upper left, with its elbow at the top-left corner
visual_elements: vertical segment forming an L-shaped bend
connotations: returning to an origin or source
navigating up and back in a hierarchy
a jump/redirect to a prior context
best_for: diagram connectors showing a path bending upward and leftward
'return to parent' or 'jump to source' navigation cues
avoid_for: simple single-axis (pure up or pure left) movement, where the plain single-direction arrows are clearer
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'reply' icon if shown small, since the L-bend shape resembles common reply/forward glyphs

name: arrow-up
denotation: a single straight arrow pointing upward
connotations: moving to a higher position or earlier item
possible_misreadings: could be confused with an 'upload' icon if paired with a tray shape, though alone it is a pure directional arrow

name: asc-sort
denotation: a downward chevron paired with an upward-pointing arrow with a shaft
visual_elements: short down-pointing chevron/arrowhead on the left
full arrow with shaft pointing up on the right
connotations: ordering values from smallest to largest
best_for: toggle buttons for ascending vs descending order
avoid_for: a generic up/down movement action unrelated to sorting/ordering data
possible_misreadings: could be confused with desc-sort at a glance since both combine similar up/down glyphs — direction of the larger arrow is the distinguishing detail

name: carret-double-left
denotation: two stacked chevrons pointing left
visual_elements: two identical V-shaped chevrons side by side, both pointing left
avoid_for: a single-step back action, where the single carret-left is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a single carret-left if rendered very small and the double chevrons merge visually

name: carret-double-right
denotation: two stacked chevrons pointing right
visual_elements: two identical V-shaped chevrons side by side, both pointing right
avoid_for: a single-step forward action, where the single carret-right is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a single carret-right if rendered very small and the double chevrons merge visually

name: carret-left
denotation: a single chevron pointing left
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open toward the right, tip pointing left
avoid_for: multi-step or 'jump to start' actions, where the double-chevron variant is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with arrow-drop-right's mirrored shape or with a plain arrow-left at small sizes

name: carret-right
denotation: a single chevron pointing right
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open toward the left, tip pointing right
best_for: expanding a side panel or tree node
avoid_for: multi-step or 'jump to end' actions, where the double-chevron variant is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with arrow-drop-right in this same set since both are right-pointing chevrons of similar weight

name: collapse
denotation: a single chevron pointing left
visual_elements: V-shaped chevron open toward the right, tip pointing left
connotations: hiding or shrinking an expanded panel
reducing a sidebar to a compact state
best_for: collapsing a sidebar or side panel to a narrow/hidden state
toggle controls paired with an 'expand' counterpart
avoid_for: a literal 'go back' page-navigation action, since visually the same chevron is used for both — context or a paired label should disambiguate
possible_misreadings: visually identical to carret-left, so out of context it could easily be read as 'previous/back' rather than 'collapse'

name: desc-sort
denotation: a downward chevron paired with a downward-pointing arrow with a shaft
visual_elements: full arrow with shaft pointing down on the right
connotations: ordering values from largest to smallest
best_for: toggle buttons for ascending vs descending order
avoid_for: a generic up/down movement action unrelated to sorting/ordering data
possible_misreadings: could be confused with asc-sort at a glance since both combine similar chevron/arrow glyphs — direction of the larger arrow is the distinguishing detail

name: enter
denotation: an arrow that travels right then curves down and back left, ending in an arrowhead, like a carriage-return symbol
visual_elements: arrowhead pointing left at the bottom
best_for: submit or confirm actions in forms and chat inputs
avoid_for: a plain directional 'go back' action, since the hook shape specifically evokes the Return key rather than simple leftward navigation
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a 'reply' or 'undo' icon since the hooked shape resembles common reply glyphs

name: expand-horizontal
denotation: two arrows pointing away from each other along a horizontal line, one left and one right
avoid_for: vertical resizing contexts, where expand-vertical is the correct icon
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'swap' or 'compare' icon if the horizontal-resize meaning isn't reinforced by a drag-handle context

name: expand-vertical
denotation: an upward arrow and a downward arrow stacked along a vertical line between two horizontal bars
connotations: increasing row height or vertical space
avoid_for: horizontal resizing contexts, where expand-horizontal is the correct icon
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'sort' or 'reorder' icon since it shares the up/down-arrow-between-bars silhouette common to sort glyphs

name: fullscreen-contract
denotation: two pairs of arrows pointing inward toward the center from opposite corners
visual_elements: arrow pointing down-left in the top area
arrow pointing up-right in the bottom area
connotations: shrinking a window back to normal size
best_for: exit-fullscreen buttons in video players or modals
restoring a maximized window/panel to its normal size
avoid_for: entering fullscreen — that is the fullscreen-expand icon, its visual opposite
possible_misreadings: could be confused with fullscreen-expand if viewed briefly, since both use the same diagonal-arrow-pair motif with only direction reversed

name: fullscreen-expand
denotation: a diagonal arrow between two opposite corners, with hooked ends suggesting the corners of a box
visual_elements: hooked corner bracket at the top-left end
hooked corner bracket at the bottom-right end
connotations: expanding content to fill the available space
best_for: enter-fullscreen buttons in video players or image viewers
avoid_for: exiting fullscreen — that is the fullscreen-contract icon, its visual opposite
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic diagonal-resize handle icon (e.g. a corner drag-resize cursor) outside of a media-player context

name: inverse
denotation: a circular loop made of two curved arrows chasing each other, one at top pointing right and one at bottom pointing left
visual_elements: circular gap between the two arcs
best_for: toggle-state controls that flip between two options
avoid_for: a literal network-sync or auto-refresh action where 'reload'/'redo2' icons in this set are more precise, since 'inverse' implies swapping two things rather than repeating one action
possible_misreadings: visually near-identical to reload/redo2 in this same icon set — the swap/invert meaning depends entirely on surrounding UI label

name: order-by
denotation: a downward arrow and an upward arrow side by side, both with shafts and arrowheads, of equal length
visual_elements: down arrow with full shaft on the left
up arrow with full shaft on the right
connotations: choosing a sort direction or ordering criterion
best_for: a menu trigger for choosing sort/order criteria
generic 'sort' buttons that open ascending/descending options
avoid_for: indicating an already-applied ascending or descending state, where asc-sort/desc-sort are more precise
possible_misreadings: could be confused with expand-vertical since both show equal-length up/down arrows, though order-by's arrows are separate rather than sharing one axis

name: redo2
denotation: a circular arrow forming most of a ring with an arrowhead at one end, like a single-direction refresh loop
visual_elements: arrowhead pointing clockwise at the top-right end of the arc
best_for: 'redo' buttons in an undo/redo history stack
avoid_for: a bidirectional sync action involving two separate flows, where the two-arrow 'reload'/'inverse' icons in this set are more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'loading/refresh' spinner icon, or with 'inverse' if viewed briefly since both are circular-arrow motifs

name: reload
denotation: two curved arrows forming a full circular loop, one arcing over the top pointing right and one arcing under the bottom pointing left
visual_elements: circular gap on both sides forming a ring
best_for: sync-status controls for connectors or pipelines
avoid_for: an undo/redo history action, where 'redo2' is the more precise single-direction icon
possible_misreadings: visually very close to 'inverse' in this same set — both are two-arrow circular loops, so their distinct meanings (refresh vs. swap) rely entirely on the accompanying label

name: reset
denotation: a counter-clockwise circular arrow
an incomplete ring with an arrowhead at the top
best_for: reverting a form or filter to its initial state
avoid_for: a single-step undo (use a plain undo/back arrow instead)
possible_misreadings: could be read as 'refresh/reload' rather than 'reset', since the glyph shape is close to a browser refresh icon

name: shrink-vertical
denotation: two arrows pointing toward each other on a horizontal divider line
top arrow pointing down and bottom arrow pointing up meeting at a center line
best_for: collapsing a panel or row height
a 'compress vertically' control in a diagram editor
possible_misreadings: could be misread as 'merge two rows' rather than a resize/shrink action

name: sync
denotation: two curved arrows forming a circular loop, one pointing right and one pointing left
connotations: keeping two things aligned over time
ongoing refresh rather than a one-time action
best_for: syncing two data sources or systems
avoid_for: a single one-off reset (use reset icon)
one-directional data flow (this reads as two-way)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'reset' or 'reload' since both use circular arrow motifs

name: uncollapse
denotation: a single chevron pointing right
connotations: opening or expanding a nested item
best_for: expanding a collapsed tree node or accordion
indicating a nested/drill-down item in a list
avoid_for: indicating a full page navigation or external link (too minimal for that)
possible_misreadings: a bare chevron alone doesn't visually distinguish 'expand' from generic 'next/forward' without surrounding UI context

name: undo
denotation: a hooked arrow curving left and down, like a U-turn arrow
connotations: stepping back to a previous state
best_for: an undo button in an editing toolbar
reversing the single most recent change
avoid_for: resetting all changes back to a default (use reset icon)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'back' navigation arrow rather than specifically 'undo'

name: unsorted
denotation: a chevron pointing up stacked above a chevron pointing down
connotations: click to choose a sort direction
best_for: a column header sort-toggle icon in a data table
indicating a field is currently unsorted
a control that cycles between ascending/descending/none
avoid_for: indicating a fixed single-direction order (use a single chevron)
expand/collapse toggles (visually similar but different intent)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for an expand/collapse ('more options') caret pair rather than a sort indicator

name: command
denotation: the Mac command key glyph — a square loop with four rounded corner loops
best_for: labeling a keyboard shortcut hint (e.g. Cmd+K)
avoid_for: Windows/Linux shortcut contexts, where Ctrl is the convention instead
possible_misreadings: users unfamiliar with Mac keyboards may not recognize this glyph as a command key at all and see only an abstract knot shape

name: control
denotation: the word 'Ctrl' rendered in a bold rounded typeface
connotations: technical/functional labeling rather than pictorial symbolism
best_for: labeling a keyboard shortcut hint (e.g. Ctrl+C)
avoid_for: macOS-specific shortcut contexts (use the Command glyph instead)
any non-keyboard-related 'control/manage' concept, despite the word 'control' overlapping in meaning
possible_misreadings: the word 'Ctrl' could be confused conceptually with 'access control' or 'controls/settings' due to the shared word, despite depicting only the literal key

name: datacatalog
denotation: a vertical rectangle (book/binder spine) with a slightly narrower vertical strip along its left edge
visual_elements: vertical divider line near the left edge forming a spine/tab
connotations: a single indexed record within a larger library
best_for: representing a data catalog or metadata repository
avoid_for: a multi-volume library or bookshelf concept (this shows a single spine, not a collection)
generic 'document' or 'file' concepts (use a page/file icon instead)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic vertical panel, sidebar toggle, or bookmark rather than specifically a catalog/book

name: caption-inside
denotation: a rectangle (frame or shape) with a short horizontal bar centered inside near the top, representing an inset label
visual_elements: short horizontal bar positioned inside the frame
connotations: a label embedded within its container
text integrated into a shape rather than floating outside it
best_for: a toggle for placing a diagram node's caption inside its shape
settings for label placement in flowcharts or org charts
avoid_for: captions that sit outside/below a shape (see caption-outside icon instead)
free-floating text annotations unconnected to a shape
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'card with title bar' UI element rather than a specific caption-placement toggle

name: caption-outside
denotation: a rectangle (shape/node) with a short horizontal bar positioned above it, outside the frame
visual_elements: short horizontal bar sitting above the frame, separated from it
connotations: a label detached from its container
external annotation kept visually distinct from the content it describes
best_for: a toggle for placing a diagram node's caption above/outside its shape
label-position settings in flowchart or diagram editors
avoid_for: captions embedded inside a shape (see caption-inside icon instead)
multi-line body text or descriptions unrelated to shape labeling
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'titled card' or 'header + box' layout icon rather than specifically an outside-caption toggle

name: circle-dashed
denotation: a circle drawn with short dashed segments instead of a solid line
connotations: a tentative, provisional, or not-yet-confirmed boundary
best_for: marking a draft, proposed, or unconfirmed diagram node
a loading or pending status ring
avoid_for: confirmed/final states (the dashed line reads as tentative, use a solid circle instead)
representing a physical object like a ring or wheel
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a loading spinner or a perforated/cuttable line rather than a 'draft' status marker

name: circle
denotation: a plain solid-outline circle
connotations: a generic node or point in a diagram
best_for: a generic diagram node or entity shape
a confirmed/complete status indicator (paired with circle-dashed as its 'draft' counterpart)
avoid_for: representing a specific object type (too generic/abstract for e.g. a sun, clock, or button)
showing partial completion or progress (use a progress ring or partial fill instead)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a letter 'O', a button, a radio input, or a clock face rather than a diagram node

name: density-high
denotation: a rounded rectangle outline above three thick horizontal bars stacked closely together
best_for: a 'compact/dense' row-height option in a table or list settings menu
toggling a data grid to show more rows per page
avoid_for: low-density/spacious layout settings (see density-low icon instead)
representing a card or panel with unrelated stacked content
possible_misreadings: could be misread as a generic 'list view' icon rather than specifically a density/spacing control, without the paired low/medium variants for contrast

name: density-low
denotation: a single thick horizontal bar inside a rounded rectangle outline
visual_elements: one thick horizontal bar centered inside
connotations: fewer items visible but easier to scan
best_for: a 'comfortable/spacious' row-height option in a table settings menu
toggling a list to show fewer, larger rows
avoid_for: high-density/compact layout settings (see density-high icon instead)
representing a single button or a plain card with a header
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a simple 'card with one line of text' or a video-player placeholder rather than a density control

name: density-medium
denotation: a thick horizontal bar with rounded ends above a separate thinner horizontal bar
best_for: a 'default/standard' row-height option in a table density settings menu, positioned between low and high density
avoid_for: representing high or low density extremes (this icon specifically signals the middle option)
a generic button-plus-caption UI element unrelated to density settings
possible_misreadings: in isolation (without the low/high siblings) could read as a generic 'pill button with subtitle' rather than a density level

name: diag-arrow-01
denotation: a single diagonal line segment running from lower-left to upper-right with no arrowhead
connotations: a plain connector or edge with no directionality
a simple link between two points
best_for: a plain, non-directional connector between two diagram nodes
avoid_for: showing directional flow or causality (no arrowhead present, use diag-arrow-02/03/04 for that)
possible_misreadings: without context could be read as a strikethrough, a fraction slash, or a 'divide' symbol rather than a diagram connector

name: diag-arrow-02
denotation: a diagonal arrow pointing to the upper-left
connotations: directional flow toward a prior or upstream point
movement or growth toward the top-left
best_for: indicating directional flow or lineage pointing upstream/backward in a diagram
avoid_for: representing growth/increase (commonly upper-right is used for that; upper-left can read ambiguously)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with diag-arrow-03/04 which point similarly but with differing angles — direction nuance may be lost at small sizes

name: diag-arrow-03
denotation: a diagonal arrow pointing to the upper-right, with a shorter shaft than diag-arrow-04
visual_elements: solid triangular arrowhead at the upper-right end
connotations: directional flow moving forward and up
best_for: showing an upward trend or growth in a metric
a directional connector pointing forward/upward in a diagram
avoid_for: precise external-link iconography if a dedicated external-link glyph is available
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for an 'external link' icon rather than a trend/growth arrow, since the two conventions look identical

name: diag-arrow-04
denotation: a diagonal arrow pointing to the upper-right with an open/outlined chevron-style arrowhead
visual_elements: open V-shaped arrowhead at the upper-right end
connotations: directional movement or growth toward the upper-right
a lighter-weight variant of a trend/flow arrow
best_for: a directional connector where a lighter arrowhead style fits the diagram's visual weight
avoid_for: contexts needing a bold, heavy arrowhead for emphasis (this one is comparatively light)
possible_misreadings: easily confused with diag-arrow-03 at small icon sizes since both point upper-right with only arrowhead style differing

name: diagram1
denotation: two small squares connected by an L-shaped line with a third square below, forming a branching flowchart
visual_elements: two squares at top connected by a horizontal bar
best_for: representing a diagram, flowchart, or data lineage view
a navigation icon for a 'diagrams' feature or module
org-chart or hierarchy visualization entry points
avoid_for: representing a single isolated data point (this specifically shows relationships between multiple nodes)
network/mesh diagrams with many interconnected nodes (this is a simple 3-node tree)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a plumbing/circuit diagram or a sitemap rather than specifically a data/process diagram without surrounding context

name: direction-after
denotation: a hollow/outlined vertical rectangle connected by an arrow pointing right into a solid arrowhead
connotations: what comes next in a sequence
a subsequent step or downstream element
forward progression from an existing item
best_for: an 'insert after' or 'move after' action in a list or sequence
indicating downstream lineage/dependency in data flow diagrams
showing the next item in an ordered sequence
avoid_for: representing what precedes an item (see direction-before)
possible_misreadings: could be misread as a generic 'export' or 'send' action rather than specifically a sequence/ordering relationship

name: direction-before
denotation: an arrow pointing right into a hollow/outlined vertical rectangle
visual_elements: outlined rectangle (target/after state) receiving the arrow
connotations: what leads up to or precedes an item
an upstream source or prior step
best_for: an 'insert before' or 'move before' action in a list or sequence
indicating upstream lineage/source in data flow diagrams
showing the prior item in an ordered sequence
avoid_for: representing what follows an item (see direction-after)
possible_misreadings: could be misread as a generic 'import' action rather than specifically a sequence/ordering relationship

name: direction-both
denotation: two arrows pointing outward in opposite directions (left and right) from a shared vertical divider
visual_elements: central dividing line they radiate from
best_for: indicating a bidirectional relationship or dependency between two items
showing a data flow or sync that goes both ways
a 'move before or after' combined control
avoid_for: one-directional flows (use direction-before or direction-after instead)
representing simultaneous unrelated actions rather than a genuine two-way relationship
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a horizontal resize/expand control rather than a directional-relationship indicator

name: eraser
denotation: an eraser drawn at a diagonal angle, wedge-shaped with a beveled corner
connotations: removing or clearing something previously drawn/entered
starting fresh by wiping content away
best_for: a 'clear canvas' or 'erase annotation' tool in a diagram/whiteboard editor
avoid_for: deleting a single discrete item like a file or record (use a trash/delete icon instead)
data cleansing/deduplication concepts (too literal an object metaphor for that)
possible_misreadings: at small sizes the wedge shape could be mistaken for a piece of chalk, a stamp, or a wedge/doorstop rather than an eraser

name: fk
denotation: a circle with a ring/hole (like a key ring) connected by a horizontal line to an arrowhead pointing right
connotations: a reference from one record to another
a key that unlocks or connects to another table
best_for: labeling a foreign key field in a database schema or ER diagram
indicating a reference/link from one table or entity to another
avoid_for: a primary key (this icon's arrow-out shape specifically suggests an outward reference, not the key itself)
generic security/lock concepts (this is schema-relationship specific, not access control)
possible_misreadings: could be misread as a generic 'key' or padlock-adjacent security icon rather than specifically a database foreign-key relationship

name: flip-horizontal
denotation: two overlapping right-triangle shapes mirrored across a vertical axis, resembling two flipped card corners
connotations: mirroring or reversing an object's orientation
best_for: a 'flip horizontal' tool button in a diagram or image editor
mirroring a selected shape or icon left-to-right
avoid_for: vertical flipping (this specifically depicts a horizontal/left-right mirror)
rotation actions (flipping is a mirror, not a rotation)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'layers' or 'duplicate shape' icon rather than specifically a mirror/flip transform

name: frame
denotation: a rectangle outlined with small cross/plus marks at each corner, like crop or frame handles
connotations: a container or artboard for grouping content
best_for: a 'create frame/artboard' tool in a canvas or diagram editor
representing a crop or selection boundary
grouping diagram elements into a defined container
avoid_for: a simple picture/photo frame in a decorative sense
representing a completed/locked shape (the crosshairs suggest an editable, adjustable boundary)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a camera focus/viewfinder icon or a crop tool rather than a diagram frame/artboard

name: full-links
denotation: a stylized Euro currency symbol made of stacked curved horizontal lines crossed by a vertical bar
best_for: representing Euro-denominated pricing, cost, or financial data
a finance/billing section icon in European-market contexts
avoid_for: non-Euro currency contexts (dollar, pound, etc. need their own glyphs)
the filename's implied meaning of 'full links' — the drawn image is a Euro sign, not a link/connector network, so it should not be used to represent linking/relationships
possible_misreadings: the filename suggests 'full-links' (a connectivity concept) but the actual glyph is unambiguously a Euro currency symbol — a significant name/image mismatch worth flagging

name: grid-magnetism-off
denotation: an ampersand-like magnet/anchor symbol with a diagonal line struck through it, indicating a disabled state
connotations: a feature turned off or disabled
best_for: toggling off grid-snap/alignment magnetism in a diagram or design canvas
avoid_for: the 'on' state of grid snapping (see grid-magnetism-on)
generic 'disabled/forbidden' contexts unrelated to grid alignment
possible_misreadings: the base glyph alone resembles an ampersand ('&') and could be misread as a text/symbol character rather than a magnetism icon if the strikethrough is missed

name: grid-magnetism-on

visual_elements: small diagonal spark marks above the magnet tips
connotations: an active pulling or attracting force
best_for: toggling on grid-snap/alignment magnetism in a diagram or design canvas
indicating that objects will snap into alignment automatically
avoid_for: physical hardware or tools content where an actual magnet is the subject rather than a UI snapping metaphor
possible_misreadings: could be read as a literal 'magnet tool' for a hardware/tools context rather than a software grid-snapping toggle

name: hand
denotation: an open hand shown palm-forward with fingers spread

avoid_for: a 'stop' or 'permission denied' warning (though the raised-palm shape could evoke that, its context here is a canvas tool, not an alert)
pointing/selection gestures (this is an open flat hand, not a pointing finger)
possible_misreadings: an open raised palm is a common 'stop' or 'halt' gesture and could be misread as a warning/blocking icon rather than a pan tool

name: handle-left
denotation: a filled circle (node) attached to a short horizontal line extending to its left
visual_elements: horizontal line stub on the left side
connotations: an anchor or connection point on the left edge of an object
an attachment site for a connector
best_for: marking a draggable connection/anchor point on the left side of a shape in a diagram editor
showing where a connector line attaches to a node
avoid_for: representing a bullet point or list marker (context here is specifically an editable anchor)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a simple bullet point with a leading dash rather than an interactive drag handle

name: handle-right
denotation: a filled circle (node) attached to a short horizontal line extending to its right
visual_elements: horizontal line stub on the right side
connotations: an anchor or connection point on the right edge of an object
an attachment site for a connector
best_for: marking a draggable connection/anchor point on the right side of a shape in a diagram editor
showing where a connector line attaches to a node
avoid_for: representing a bullet point or list marker (context here is specifically an editable anchor)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a simple bullet point with a trailing dash rather than an interactive drag handle

name: handle-simple
denotation: a single small filled circle/dot
connotations: a generic anchor or control point with no directional stub
a basic unit or single item
best_for: a plain drag/resize handle on a shape with no directional line
a bullet point marker in a list
a generic status dot (e.g. online/active indicator)
avoid_for: a handle that needs to convey a specific direction or side (see handle-left/handle-right for that)
representing a fully bounded/outlined entity (see the plain circle icon, which is a ring, not filled)
possible_misreadings: a plain filled dot is highly generic and could be read as a bullet, a period, a status light, or a radio button depending on context

name: light-links
denotation: a single curved line splitting into two branches from one point
visual_elements: one stem entering from the right
curved fork into two prongs on the left
connotations: a path dividing or branching outward
one thing giving rise to two related things
a lightweight, unadorned link compared to node-and-dot diagrams
best_for: showing one source splitting into two outputs
toggle for a 'light' or minimal link-rendering mode
avoid_for: representing a two-way or bidirectional relationship (shape is one-directional-looking)
showing merging of two into one (visually reads as splitting, not merging)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a wishbone or slingshot shape
could be mistaken for a simple check/tick mark at a glance due to the curve

name: lineage-explore
denotation: two small squares connected by a branching line to a larger square
a plus sign attached to the bottom-left square
visual_elements: small square bottom-left with an overlaid plus sign
connotations: expanding outward from a node to discover more connections
adding new nodes to a data-lineage graph
growth of a network by exploration
best_for: a button/action to expand or explore further lineage nodes
avoid_for: static/read-only lineage display (the plus implies an active add/expand action)
possible_misreadings: the plus sign could be read generically as 'add new item' unrelated to lineage if context is stripped away

name: lineage-group
denotation: a rounded rectangle bracketed by horizontal tick marks on both sides
visual_elements: two short horizontal lines on the left
two short horizontal lines on the right
connotations: a cluster or bundle of items collapsed into a single unit
consolidation of multiple inputs/outputs into one grouped object
best_for: toggling a 'group' view of lineage nodes
representing an aggregated/collapsed set of related items
avoid_for: representing a single ungrouped data field (implies plurality collapsed together)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a USB/connector plug icon due to the pill-with-prongs shape
could read as a battery or capacitor symbol out of context

name: lineage-static
denotation: two small squares connected by a branching line to a larger rounded square, no plus sign
connotations: a fixed, unchanging snapshot of a lineage graph
a simple three-node relationship without further interaction
best_for: distinguishing a fixed diagram mode from an explorable one
avoid_for: depicting a live/real-time or dynamically updating relationship

name: lineage-ungroup
denotation: a single square branching out to two squares of the same size
visual_elements: two equally-sized squares top-right and center-right
connotations: splitting a single grouped item back into its separate constituent parts
decomposition or breaking apart a bundle
expansion of one node into multiple distinct nodes
best_for: an action button to ungroup previously clustered lineage nodes
showing decomposition of an aggregate into individual elements
avoid_for: showing merging or combining of nodes (this is the reverse operation)
possible_misreadings: visually close to 'lineage-explore'/'lineage-group' family — the distinguishing detail (equal-sized squares, no plus) is subtle at 128px

name: maxi-obj
denotation: a rounded rectangle with three horizontal lines of text inside it
connotations: a large or detailed content card/object with substantial text
a document or record shown in full detail
best_for: a toggle/option for the largest object display density in a catalog or diagram view
representing a content-rich card versus a compact one
avoid_for: representing an empty or minimal object (the three text lines imply populated content)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'notes' or 'list' icon without the medium/mini size context

name: medium-obj
denotation: a plain empty rounded rectangle outline
connotations: a mid-sized, moderately detailed object card
a balanced or default display setting between minimal and maximal
an empty container ready to hold content
best_for: a toggle/option for medium object display density in catalog or diagram views
avoid_for: representing text-rich or detailed content (it shows no internal detail)
possible_misreadings: extremely generic shape — without surrounding size-variant siblings (mini/medium/maxi) it could be read as any blank rectangle icon, e.g. a button or input field

name: mini-obj
denotation: a small rounded square outline, thicker stroke relative to its size
connotations: the smallest/most compact object display option
a minimized or collapsed representation of a data node
best_for: a toggle/option for the smallest object display density
a compact/icon-only node representation in dense diagrams
avoid_for: showing detailed or text-rich content (too small/plain to imply detail)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'stop' or 'square' button icon out of the size-variant context

name: more-obj

visual_elements: badge positioned at bottom-right corner overlap
connotations: adding an additional object or card to a collection
expanding a set with one more item
a create/append action tied to a card-shaped entity
best_for: a button to add a new object/card to a diagram or catalog
indicating additional/more items available beyond what's shown
avoid_for: representing deletion or removal (the badge is a plus, not a minus)

name: nano-box
denotation: a single small solid black dot/circle
visual_elements: one filled circle, no outline shape around it
connotations: the absolute minimum unit — a single point with no further detail
a bullet or marker denoting presence rather than content
best_for: representing the smallest possible zoom/density level in a diagram (below mini/medium/maxi)
a generic bullet or status dot marker
avoid_for: conveying any object type or content detail (it's an undifferentiated dot)
possible_misreadings: easily confused with a plain bullet point, a status indicator dot, or a period/full-stop glyph

name: photo
denotation: a camera body with a circular lens in the center
visual_elements: circular lens ring in the middle
connotations: visual/photographic media as distinct from text data
best_for: an action to upload or take a photo
labeling image-type content or media attachments
avoid_for: representing video or audio media (camera specifically implies still photography)

name: pk
denotation: a physical door key with a round bow and two teeth on the shaft
visual_elements: circular key bow (head) with a hole
two rectangular teeth/notches at the shaft end
connotations: unique identification or unlocking access to a specific record
the defining/master attribute of a data record
authority or exclusive access to something
best_for: marking a primary key column/field in a database table view
avoid_for: representing a foreign key or a non-unique attribute (a plain key visually implies primacy/uniqueness, not reference)
possible_misreadings: could be read generically as 'security', 'login', or 'password' rather than specifically 'primary key' without table context

name: relation
denotation: an S-shaped curved line connecting two small circles at opposite corners
connotations: a flowing, organic link between two distinct entities
an established relationship or dependency between two data objects
best_for: indicating a relationship/link exists between two data objects or tables
a generic 'connect' action button between entities
avoid_for: showing a one-to-many or hierarchical relationship (shape reads as a simple peer-to-peer link)

name: selection
denotation: a solid filled mouse-pointer/cursor arrow shape
visual_elements: solid black fill, no outline detail
connotations: actively pointing at or choosing a specific element
focus on a single selected target
best_for: a selection-tool button in a diagram/canvas editor
avoid_for: representing navigation/direction (a directional arrow reads differently from a mouse cursor)

name: unrelation

visual_elements: diagonal strike-through slash across the curve
connotations: breaking or removing an existing link between two entities
best_for: an action to remove/delete a relationship between two data objects
indicating a broken or invalid link
avoid_for: showing an active/intact relationship (this specifically signals absence or removal of one)

name: zoom-reset

visual_elements: empty space between them where a full frame would close
connotations: returning a view to its default or original framing
resetting zoom/crop back to a baseline extent
a viewport or bounding frame in transition

name: catalog

diagonal edge lines converging at the center top vertex
connotations: a structured, multi-faceted collection of items
a 'package' or bounded set of assets viewed as a whole
best_for: labeling a data catalog or master inventory section
representing a structured collection of governed assets
avoid_for: representing a single flat document or a 2D list (the cube implies dimensional structure/depth)

connotations: organizing content into side-by-side vertical panels

visual_elements: two evenly spaced vertical divider lines
two evenly spaced horizontal divider lines
connotations: a structured holder made of many equal sub-compartments
an organizational grid for grouping many similar items
best_for: representing a container or schema object that holds multiple sub-elements
avoid_for: representing a single unstructured item or an empty/plain box (the grid implies multiple compartments)

name: containerfolder
denotation: a classic file folder shape with a small tab cut at the top-left corner

a directory-like holder in a file system
best_for: representing a folder-like container that groups other objects
navigation elements for a directory/hierarchy tree
avoid_for: representing a single file (the tab/folder silhouette specifically implies containment of multiple items)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'save' or 'archive' icon without folder-hierarchy context

name: csv-file

visual_elements: bold 'CSV' letters beneath the page shape
connotations: tabular, comma-separated data stored in a plain file
a specific, recognizable file-type identity rather than a generic document
best_for: labeling or downloading a CSV-formatted file
distinguishing CSV file type in a list of data sources
avoid_for: representing other file formats like JSON, XML, or database tables (text label explicitly says CSV)

name: database

database infrastructure combined with descriptive metadata
a system holding both storage and reference material
best_for: representing a database connection or data source object
a data-store entry paired with documentation/metadata
avoid_for: representing a single flat file with no storage-system context
possible_misreadings: could be read as generic 'server/rack' hardware rather than specifically a logical database

name: datafield
denotation: two stacked bar shapes with a curly-brace pair ‘{}’ overlapping at bottom-right

parameters or properties nested within a larger structure
best_for: representing an individual data field/attribute within a table or schema
denoting a code-structured or JSON-like property
avoid_for: representing a whole table or database (this is a single-field/attribute-level icon)

name: ddl

a formal, rigid specification (as opposed to loose/free-form data)

badge overlapping bottom-right corner of grid
connotations: equipment or asset tagged with a specific identifying number
a numbered/serialized physical or technical item
structured inventory entries with unique reference codes
best_for: representing an equipment/asset object identified by a numeric code
tagging technical/physical inventory entries in a catalog

name: file-empty

connotations: a blank, unpopulated file or document
a generic placeholder for any file type
a starting point before content is added
best_for: representing an empty/new file before content is added
a generic fallback icon for any unspecified file type
avoid_for: representing a specific file format (CSV, SQL, etc. have their own labeled icons) or a populated/detailed record

name: filestore

connotations: a storage location holding discrete files rather than structured tables
a repository combining storage infrastructure with file-level content
best_for: representing a file-storage data source (e.g. object storage, file share) in a source catalog

avoid_for: representing a structured relational database (that role belongs to the 'database' icon with a document badge of different shape)

name: nosql

connotations: layered, flexible storage without rigid tabular structure
horizontal stacking suggesting document or key-value layers
a schema-less or loosely structured data store
best_for: representing a NoSQL/document-oriented database connection or source type

avoid_for: representing a strictly relational/SQL table (no grid/column structure is shown, deliberately)

name: source

connotations: an originating document that anchors or grounds downstream data

best_for: representing the originating source document/file in a lineage or provenance chain

name: sub-structure

name: table

name: tagbase

avoid_for: representing a single tagged field/item (that's the plain 'tag' icon) — this is source/base-level

name: view

visibility, inspection, or a derived/virtual perspective on a data source

best_for: representing a database VIEW object (virtual/derived table) in a schema catalog
denoting visibility/inspection actions on a data source
avoid_for: representing a physical base table that stores its own data directly (a view is derived, not primary storage)

name: expand-all

connotations: expanding content outward to fill more space
opening up or revealing everything at once

a maximize/fullscreen toggle for a panel or canvas

name: extension

two concave notches on bottom edge
connotations: something that plugs into a larger whole
avoid_for: core/native features (puzzle piece implies bolt-on, not built-in)

name: concept
denotation: an open incomplete circle / arc with two rounded ends

name: dimension-group

avoid_for: a single dimension (this icon's four-arrow form implies plurality/multiple axes, not one)

name: dimension

name: domain

name: indicator-group

best_for: a grouped set of KPIs or indicators

name: indicator
denotation: a single wavy line resembling a tilde inside a circle
connotations: a single approximate or variable measure
best_for: a single individual indicator or KPI

name: reference-data

avoid_for: free-form or narrative content (this is a structured list glyph, implying discrete itemized entries)
possible_misreadings: generic enough to be read as a plain UI 'list view' or 'menu' icon rather than specifically reference data

name: term
denotation: three stacked horizontal bars/lines inside a circle

best_for: generic text or paragraph content indicator
avoid_for: itemized/enumerated lists specifically (no bullets here, so it reads as prose/paragraph, not a list)
possible_misreadings: nearly identical to a generic 'menu' (hamburger-style) icon or 'text/paragraph' icon out of context

name: universe
denotation: a bullseye / concentric double ring

best_for: the top-level 'universe' or overall scope container in a data taxonomy
avoid_for: a small/specific sub-item (bullseye connotes the encompassing whole, not a granular leaf node)
possible_misreadings: easily read as a 'target/goal' or 'focus' icon rather than a taxonomy-scope concept

name: value
denotation: a minus/dash sign inside a circle

avoid_for: addition/growth concepts (the minus form specifically implies subtraction or a flat single value, not increase)
possible_misreadings: strongly resembles a generic 'minus/remove' or 'collapse' UI icon rather than a data value concept

name: governance

visual_elements: small void/hole off-center in the needle
avoid_for: a specific granular rule or single policy (this icon reads as the overarching governance/steering function, not one rule)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a plain map/travel compass rather than a governance-specific icon

name: policy-external
denotation: a bookmark ribbon shape with two horizontal text lines and a small flag/tail
connotations: a tagged or flagged official document
something marked for reference from outside
best_for: policies originating outside the organization (regulatory/external requirements)
avoid_for: internal-only policy content (the external framing/tag connotation is specific)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'save/bookmark' UI action rather than specifically an external policy

name: policy-group
denotation: an empty bookmark ribbon outline
visual_elements: ribbon/bookmark outline with notched V-shaped bottom
connotations: an official but content-less placeholder/folder for policies
marking something as important to return to

name: policy-lifecycle

visual_elements: two opposing curved arrows forming a refresh/cycle loop
connotations: a policy that evolves through stages over time
avoid_for: a static, one-time, never-updated policy (the cycle arrows specifically connote recurring change)

name: policy-masking

name: policy-validation
denotation: a bookmark ribbon containing a checkmark

name: policy
denotation: a shield containing a solid triangle

avoid_for: a soft/optional guideline (the shield-and-triangle combo reads as a hard enforced protection, not a suggestion)

name: rule-ethics
denotation: a shield with two horizontal text lines
visual_elements: two horizontal bars stacked inside representing text/document lines
connotations: a documented standard of right conduct
avoid_for: technical/security-specific rules (this icon's plain text-lines-in-shield motif is generic and doesn't carry a security-specific signal like a lock would)
possible_misreadings: visually very close to other 'shield + lines' governance icons (rule-lifecycle, rule) — differentiated mainly by the two flat bars vs. other symbols inside

name: rule-lifecycle
denotation: a shield containing two curved cycle/refresh arrows
visual_elements: two opposing curved arrows forming a loop
connotations: a rule that is periodically reviewed or renewed
avoid_for: a permanent, never-revised rule (the refresh arrows specifically signal recurring change)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with the policy-lifecycle bookmark icon — differ only in outer shape (shield vs. ribbon)

name: rule-privacy
denotation: a shield containing a person's silhouette (head and shoulders)

avoid_for: organization-wide or non-personal data rules (the human silhouette specifically signals individual/personal protection)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'account security' or 'user profile' icon without the privacy-specific rule context

name: rule-quality
denotation: a shield containing a checkmark
connotations: a passed quality check enforced as a rule
avoid_for: failed or pending quality checks (the checkmark specifically implies a passed state)
possible_misreadings: nearly identical silhouette to policy-validation (checkmark-in-shape) — differ mainly by outer shield vs. bookmark shape

name: rule-security
denotation: a shield containing a padlock

possible_misreadings: very legible as security; low risk of misreading, though could generically be read as 'account security' without the rule-specific context

name: rule
denotation: a shield containing two horizontal text lines
best_for: generic rule/guideline content within a governance framework
avoid_for: rules with a specific sub-type already covered by a dedicated icon (ethics, security, privacy, quality) since this is the generic base version
possible_misreadings: visually nearly identical to rule-ethics (same shield + two-lines motif) — the two are effectively indistinguishable without context/label

name: rules-group
denotation: an empty shield outline
connotations: the umbrella under which specific rules sit
best_for: a folder/group containing multiple specific rules
generic protection or security branding without a specific sub-theme
avoid_for: a single specific rule with defined content (this is the empty/generic container, not a detailed rule)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a plain 'security' or 'protected' badge unrelated to rule grouping specifically

name: activity-log

visual_elements: clock hands pointing to roughly 10 and 2
curved arrow wrapping around the left side with an arrowhead
best_for: version history or 'restore previous state' actions
avoid_for: future scheduling or upcoming events (the backward-wrapping arrow specifically implies looking to the past)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a plain 'undo' or 'restore' button rather than specifically an activity log

name: add

visual_elements: intersecting at center forming a cross
avoid_for: removal or reduction concepts (directly opposite meaning to this icon's addition signal)

avoid_for: linear/sequential processes (this icon's radial hub structure implies distributed relationships, not a straight sequence)

name: api

best_for: filter/sort UI controls repurposed for asset browsing
avoid_for: equal-weighted/unranked collections (the decreasing bar lengths specifically imply hierarchy or filtering, not a flat list)

avoid_for: literal lighting/electrical hardware content (in a software context this is almost always metaphorical, not literal)

denotation: a scalloped/wavy-edged badge or seal shape with a checkmark inside

avoid_for: a literal marketing megaphone/announcement concept (despite the filename 'campaign', it depicts a certification seal, not a bullhorn)

name: cancelsearch

best_for: representing editing or annotating items in a collection/tray
a content authoring or CDP (content/data platform) feature icon
avoid_for: a pure delete or trash action (tray is open-topped like an inbox, not a trash can)
literal customer-data-platform branding without an editing context, since the icon reads as generic edit-in-tray

second speech bubble outline peeking behind it

avoid_for: a single standalone comment (this icon implies multiple/ongoing exchange via the two overlapping bubbles)

avoid_for: a generic comment or answered-message context (the question mark specifically signals an open/unresolved query)

representing a dashboard of widgets or content blocks

an IT asset or workstation icon
