name: logo-dg
denotation: a lumpy cloud shape made of overlapping circles
avoid_for: representing the DataGalaxy company logo/brand mark itself, despite the filename — this is a generic cloud glyph with no wordmark or brand-specific shape
possible_misreadings: could be read as a sheep or animal silhouette due to the bumpy outline
could be mistaken for a generic 'weather' icon

name: mandatory
denotation: an exclamation point inside a circle
best_for: marking required fields or mandatory steps
avoid_for: depicting a hard error or failure state (it reads as emphasis/requirement, not a caution triangle or stop sign)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'info' or 'alert' icon since exclamation-in-circle is a broadly shared glyph

name: mapping
denotation: three dots connected by two lines in a branching pattern, like a share icon
visual_elements: two dots on the right (upper and lower)
connecting line segments forming a Y/fork shape
connotations: linking one thing to several others
distribution or fan-out from a source
avoid_for: depicting a literal geographic map or cartographic content — nothing in the glyph resembles a map, pin, or territory
possible_misreadings: easily mistaken for a generic 'share' icon rather than a data-mapping concept, since the shape is identical to common share glyphs

name: marker
denotation: a location pin with a dot in its center
visual_elements: circular dot in the widest part
best_for: indicating a physical location or address
marking a point of reference on a map or diagram
avoid_for: abstract data pinning/bookmarking use cases where a real-world location isn't implied — the pin shape strongly implies geography

name: minus-circle
denotation: a horizontal minus sign inside a circle
avoid_for: representing a hard delete/destroy action (reads as gentle removal, not destructive delete like a trash can)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'no entry' or prohibition sign if the bar is misread as a diagonal slash

name: minus
denotation: a single horizontal bar with rounded ends
avoid_for: contexts needing a clear 'delete' connotation — this is too abstract/minimal to read as destructive removal
possible_misreadings: without context could be read as a hyphen, an underline fragment, or a progress-bar segment rather than a mathematical minus

name: more
denotation: three horizontally arranged solid dots
visual_elements: three evenly spaced filled circles in a row
avoid_for: a vertical three-dot 'kebab' menu specifically, since this is the horizontal variant and could mismatch platform conventions if that distinction matters
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a loading/progress indicator (three dots animation) rather than a menu trigger

name: multi-value
denotation: a price/offer tag with a plus sign attached
a tag shape with a small circle hole and a filled dot
visual_elements: tag/label outline with pointed left edge
small punch-hole circle near the point
plus sign badge overlapping the bottom right
connotations: expanding a set of labeled items
best_for: adding a new tag or attribute value
avoid_for: single fixed labeling with no 'add more' implication — the plus badge specifically signals an add action
possible_misreadings: could be read as a price-tag icon (e-commerce) rather than a metadata/tagging concept without surrounding context

name: new-window
denotation: an open rectangular frame with a diagonal arrow exiting its top-right corner
visual_elements: diagonal arrow pointing up and to the right
best_for: links that open a new tab/window
avoid_for: a general 'expand/fullscreen' action within the same context — the missing corner and outward arrow specifically imply leaving to a new frame
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'send' or 'export' arrow icon if the frame outline isn't noticed

name: no-notification
denotation: a bell shape with a diagonal line struck through it
visual_elements: diagonal slash crossing the entire bell
avoid_for: representing notifications being actively received — this specifically communicates absence/suppression, the opposite state

name: none
denotation: a circle with a diagonal line through it, like a 'no entry' or prohibition sign
visual_elements: single diagonal slash from lower-left to upper-right
best_for: showing a blocked, disabled, or prohibited state or permission
avoid_for: a soft 'remove one item' action (that's better served by minus-circle) — this reads as a harder prohibition/negation
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'no smoking'-style universal prohibition sign for an unrelated specific restriction rather than a generic null/none value

name: note
denotation: a square sheet of paper with its bottom-right corner folded/dog-eared
visual_elements: folded corner triangle at bottom right
best_for: attaching a note or comment to an item
avoid_for: representing a full multi-page document or file (better served by a stack-of-pages icon) — this is a single folded sheet
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'file' or 'document' icon rather than specifically a note/memo

name: notification
denotation: a bell shape without a clapper line
visual_elements: small rounded base/clapper bump at bottom
avoid_for: a muted/off state (that's the no-notification variant with the slash)

name: number
denotation: the digits '123' in bold rounded type
avoid_for: representing currency or measurement units specifically (it's a generic numeral glyph, not a $ or % symbol)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a step-sequence indicator (step 1-2-3) rather than a data-type marker depending on context

name: object
denotation: a plain empty rounded square outline
best_for: representing a generic data object, entity, or item slot
avoid_for: conveying any specific meaning beyond 'a generic thing' — being maximally abstract, it carries no distinguishing semantic signal on its own
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a stop/pause button, a checkbox, or a photo placeholder frame since a bare square is highly ambiguous

name: online
denotation: a lumpy cloud shape made of overlapping circles, identical in form to logo-dg
avoid_for: distinguishing an 'offline' state — this glyph carries no strikethrough or dimming to contrast against an offline counterpart
possible_misreadings: visually indistinguishable from a plain 'cloud' icon (e.g. logo-dg) — 'online' meaning here relies entirely on context/label, not on the shape itself

name: organization
denotation: three ring/donut shapes of different sizes plus one small solid dot, arranged loosely
connotations: a cluster of related but distinct entities
a network of nodes at different scales
best_for: representing an organization, department cluster, or group of related nodes
avoid_for: depicting a strict top-down hierarchy with clear parent/child lines — there are no connecting lines here, only loosely arranged shapes
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a molecular/atomic diagram or a bubble chart rather than an organizational concept

name: pending
denotation: an hourglass/timer shape
visual_elements: two triangular halves meeting at a narrow waist
avoid_for: a precisely scheduled/calendar-based time reference (better served by a clock or calendar icon) — hourglass implies duration/waiting, not a specific point in time
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a bowtie or abstract geometric shape if not recognized as an hourglass at small sizes

name: person
denotation: a single human figure — round head above a rounded shoulder/torso arc
best_for: denoting an individual owner, steward, or contact
avoid_for: representing a group or team (this is explicitly a single figure, not multiple people)

name: plus-circle
denotation: a plus sign inside a circle
visual_elements: horizontal bar crossing it at center
avoid_for: representing medical/first-aid content (a common alternate reading of plus signs) — no other visual cues here support that reading
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a medical cross icon if isolated from UI context

name: preferences
denotation: two horizontal sliders with round handles at different positions on each track
visual_elements: top track with a filled circle handle positioned left-of-center
bottom track with a filled circle handle positioned right-of-center
best_for: settings or preferences screens with adjustable sliders
avoid_for: a simple on/off toggle switch (this shows slider tracks with positioned handles, implying a range, not a binary state)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for an audio equalizer or filter-adjustment icon rather than general preferences

name: process-old
denotation: two arrows crossing each other in an X/shuffle pattern
visual_elements: two curved arrow paths crossing mid-way
best_for: depicting a swap or crossover between two flows
avoid_for: depicting a straightforward linear/sequential process (this specifically implies crossing/exchange, not a simple forward flow)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'sync' or 'refresh' icon due to the curved arrow style

name: project
denotation: a ringed planet, like Saturn, with an orbit line and a curved line beneath suggesting a smile or base shadow
best_for: project or initiative branding, especially with a space/galaxy visual theme
avoid_for: literal astronomy or science-education content expecting an accurate planetary depiction — this is a stylized generic ringed-planet glyph
possible_misreadings: could be read as a UFO, a hat, or an abstract ring-and-ball shape rather than specifically 'project'

name: quality
denotation: a medal or badge with a checkmark, hanging from a ribbon
avoid_for: representing a simple pass/fail checkbox (the medal/ribbon framing specifically implies achievement or certification, more ceremonial than a plain checkmark)

name: recent
denotation: a clock face with a counterclockwise arrow wrapping around it, and a downward arrowhead
visual_elements: clock hands pointing to a time
curved arrow around the circle's top
best_for: version history or 'restore previous state' actions
avoid_for: a forward-looking 'schedule for later' feature — the arrow direction here implies looking backward, not forward in time
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'refresh/reload' icon since both use a circular arrow around a shape

name: richtext
denotation: the letters 'txt' with an X marked through the letters, above a horizontal underline
connotations: written content as a data type
best_for: labeling a text/string data field or rich-text editor feature
avoid_for: plain unformatted text specifically — 'richtext' implies styling/formatting capability beyond bare text
possible_misreadings: at small sizes the 'txt' letterforms could be misread as an abstract pattern rather than legible text

name: save
denotation: a floppy disk with a folded top-right corner
best_for: save/commit actions in forms and editors
avoid_for: depicting cloud-based or auto-save mechanisms (the floppy disk specifically evokes manual, local, legacy saving)
possible_misreadings: younger audiences unfamiliar with floppy disks may not immediately recognize this as 'save' and could see it as a generic panel/card shape

name: screen-settings
denotation: a browser/window frame with a gear badge overlapping its bottom-right corner
visual_elements: top bar line suggesting a browser chrome/tab area
gear/cog icon badge at bottom right
connotations: configuring a display, app, or interface
avoid_for: a generic standalone settings action unrelated to a screen/window/app context (plain 'settings' gear alone conveys that better)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a plain browser/app icon if the small gear badge isn't noticed at reduced sizes

name: search
denotation: a magnifying glass
visual_elements: diagonal handle extending from lower right
best_for: search bars and search action buttons
avoid_for: representing a zoom function specifically, even though magnifying glasses are sometimes reused for zoom (context should disambiguate)
possible_misreadings: could occasionally be read as a 'zoom in' control rather than search, depending on surrounding UI

name: send
denotation: a triangular paper airplane / send arrow pointing toward the upper right
visual_elements: folded internal line suggesting a paper airplane crease
angled point in the direction of travel
avoid_for: representing receiving/inbox actions — direction and framing strongly imply outbound sending

name: sensor-external
denotation: a rounded rectangle with three vertical signal bars inside and a short stem/line beneath
visual_elements: three vertical bars of varying height inside
small stem line extending below the rectangle
connotations: a device receiving/emitting signals from outside itself
best_for: external/third-party data source or sensor references
avoid_for: representing an internal-only sensor without any external connection implication — the stem line here specifically suggests an external connector/antenna
possible_misreadings: visually very close to sensor-internal and sensor-group; the stem line is the only distinguishing feature and could be missed at small sizes

name: sensor-group
denotation: a rounded rectangle with three vertical signal bars inside, no external stem
visual_elements: three vertical bars of varying height inside, centered
connotations: a self-contained cluster of signal sources
a monitoring panel or dashboard device
best_for: dashboard/panel icons showing aggregated signal data
avoid_for: a single isolated data point (the multi-bar graphic implies plurality/grouping, not one discrete reading)
possible_misreadings: nearly identical to sensor-internal and sensor-external; without labels these three are very hard to tell apart

name: sensor-internal
denotation: a wide rounded rectangle with three vertical signal bars inside, shorter and squatter than the other sensor variants
visual_elements: three vertical bars of varying height inside, closer to the top edge
avoid_for: external data source connections (see sensor-external, which specifically adds the stem line for that)
possible_misreadings: visually near-identical to sensor-group and sensor-external — proportions differ only slightly, easy to confuse without side-by-side comparison

name: settings
denotation: a gear/cog with a circle in its center
avoid_for: representing a specific single adjustable value (better served by preferences' slider glyph) — the gear implies general system configuration, not a specific tunable parameter

name: share
denotation: a curved arrow bending upward and to the right, like a reply or redirect arrow
visual_elements: arrowhead pointing up and to the right
best_for: share buttons in apps and social contexts
avoid_for: depicting a reciprocal/mutual relationship between two parties (this arrow is one-directional, better suited to an outbound share/forward than a two-way exchange)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a 'reply' or 'undo' icon since curved arrows are visually similar across those meanings

name: spinner
denotation: an incomplete circular arc with rounded open ends
connotations: something in motion or being computed
best_for: indicating a loading or in-progress state
showing a background process is running
avoid_for: anything requiring precise progress percentage (this shows no fill amount)
possible_misreadings: could read as a partial letter C or crescent shape if not animated
could be mistaken for an incomplete circle chart

name: splitter
denotation: three vertically stacked solid dots
connotations: more options hidden behind a menu
avoid_for: a bulleted list (too generic/small for that)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a vertical ellipsis/more-menu rather than a 'splitter' if unlabeled
could be mistaken for a loading dots animation

name: star-fav
denotation: a single solid filled five-pointed star
best_for: marking a favorited or starred item
avoid_for: representing a rating scale of multiple stars (this is a single icon, not a 1-5 scale)
an unselected/toggle-off favorite state (use the outline variant instead)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with an award/badge if context is unclear

name: star
denotation: an outlined five-pointed star, unfilled
connotations: an available action to mark something
best_for: an empty rating star in a rating widget
avoid_for: signaling something is already marked/favorited (use the filled star-fav for that)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with an award or sheriff-badge outline in isolation

name: tag-hierarchy
denotation: two overlapping price/label tags, one with a hole
avoid_for: a single flat tag with no notion of hierarchy or grouping (a single-tag icon would fit better)
possible_misreadings: could be read as two price tags for e-commerce/pricing rather than data tagging

name: team
denotation: three small circular heads above one larger rounded body, a group-of-people glyph
best_for: representing a team or group of users
avoid_for: a single individual user (too crowded/plural for that)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'community' or 'audience' icon depending on context

name: text
denotation: the literal lowercase letters 't x t' spelling 'txt'
best_for: labeling a text field or text data type
avoid_for: rich formatted text/typography (this reads as plain/raw text specifically)
possible_misreadings: could be misread as a multiplication or math symbol icon due to the 'x' shape if cropped tightly

name: text1
denotation: a single capital letter T with a rounded serif-like base stroke
best_for: a text-insertion or typography tool button
avoid_for: representing full paragraphs of body text (better suited to a single character/title context)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a cross/plus-like construction tool icon rather than a text glyph if very small

name: time-series
denotation: four vertical bars of increasing height forming a rising bar chart
best_for: representing time-series data or trend charts
avoid_for: a declining/negative trend (bars only ascend here)
non-sequential or categorical comparisons with no time dimension
possible_misreadings: could be read generically as any bar chart rather than specifically time-series data without context

name: unlink
denotation: a broken chain-like curve with a gap and small floating dash fragments
best_for: an action to remove a link/association between two data objects
showing a broken or severed relationship
avoid_for: representing an intact/active link (this specifically shows breakage)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic error/glitch icon due to the scattered dash fragments

name: unlock
denotation: a padlock with its shackle open, swung to one side
best_for: an action to grant permissions or remove a restriction
avoid_for: representing a secured/restricted state (opposite meaning — use a closed padlock for that)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a padlock that is simply mid-toggle rather than a permanent unlocked state

name: upload
denotation: a downward-pointing arrow above a horizontal tray/bracket shape
best_for: an upload or import action button
avoid_for: an 'export/send outward' action, since the arrow direction here reads as inward/downward into a tray rather than outward
possible_misreadings: the downward arrow into a tray is the conventional 'download' glyph in many other icon sets — could be swapped with download depending on convention; verify against this set's own upload/download pairing

name: view-list
denotation: four stacked horizontal bars of equal length
best_for: toggling to a list display mode
representing a menu or itemized list
avoid_for: a grid/card display mode (opposite layout paradigm)
hierarchical/tree data (flat bars imply a flat list, not nesting)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a hamburger navigation menu icon rather than a list-view toggle depending on placement

name: view-tree
denotation: three horizontal bars of decreasing length arranged like a staggered indent
best_for: toggling to a hierarchical/tree display mode
representing nested or indented data structures
avoid_for: a flat, non-hierarchical list (use view-list instead)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a simple decreasing bar chart rather than an indentation/hierarchy cue if not paired with view-list for contrast

name: visual-analysis
denotation: a network of connected circles of varying sizes, resembling nodes and edges
visual_elements: four connected circular nodes of different sizes
best_for: representing lineage/relationship graphs between data objects
avoid_for: simple linear or tabular data views (this implies non-linear, branching relationships)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a molecule/chemistry diagram icon out of context

name: warning
denotation: a triangle outline containing an exclamation mark
connotations: an alert that something may go wrong
best_for: flagging warnings, risks, or data quality issues
avoid_for: a hard failure/blocking error (a triangle warning typically implies caution, not fatal failure — a stop/octagon or red X may fit fatal errors better)

name: my-work
denotation: an open inbox tray shape with a curved dip in the middle
connotations: a collection point for one's own tasks
best_for: a 'My Work' navigation entry or personal task inbox
avoid_for: a shared/team-wide inbox (this reads as an individual, personal tray)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic archive or storage box icon without the 'my work' label context

name: hide
denotation: an eye shape with a diagonal line struck through it
best_for: a toggle to hide content or make it invisible
avoid_for: a 'show/view' action (opposite meaning — use the plain eye icon for that)

name: object-page
denotation: a rounded rectangular card outline with a horizontal bar across its lower third
visual_elements: horizontal divider bar near the bottom
connotations: a structured record or detail page
best_for: representing a data object's detail/profile page
navigation to a single record's dedicated view
avoid_for: a list or grid of many objects (this specifically denotes a single page/card)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic browser window or a simple card/badge icon out of context

name: object-preview
denotation: a rounded document/card shape with a magnifying glass overlapping its bottom-left corner
connotations: a quick look without full navigation
best_for: a 'preview' or 'quick view' action on a data object
inspecting content without opening the full page
avoid_for: a full-text search across a whole catalog (the magnifier here is paired with a single object, implying inspection of one item, not a global search)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a plain search icon if the document shape isn't noticed

name: show
denotation: an open eye shape with a filled circular pupil
avoid_for: a 'hide' or privacy-off action (opposite meaning — use the crossed-out eye for that)

name: view-more
denotation: an open eye shape with a small plus sign attached at its lower-right
best_for: an action to start watching/following an item
avoid_for: a simple 'show' toggle with no added/expand connotation (use the plain eye icon for that)
possible_misreadings: the plus badge could be misread as 'add new item' rather than 'expand/watch' without surrounding context

name: watch-on
denotation: a solid filled eye shape
best_for: indicating an active 'watching/following' state on an item
a filled-state companion icon to an outline 'watch' toggle
avoid_for: a neutral or off/unwatched state (the solid fill implies active engagement)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'show/view' icon since it's also just an eye shape, distinguished mainly by its solid fill style

name: admin
denotation: a shield outline containing a small person silhouette
best_for: representing an administrator or privileged-user role
an access-control/permissions feature tied to a specific person
avoid_for: a generic/non-privileged user (the shield specifically implies elevated protection or authority)
possible_misreadings: could be read broadly as 'security' or 'protected account' rather than specifically 'admin' without a label

name: invite-user
denotation: a person silhouette (circular head, rounded body) with a plus sign attached at the lower right
best_for: an 'invite a new user' or 'add member' action button
avoid_for: removing a user (opposite action — would need a minus/remove variant)

name: personal-doc
denotation: a document page with a folded top-right corner and a person silhouette in the lower-right
connotations: a file attributed to or authored by a specific person
best_for: representing a document tied to a specific owner/author
a personal-files section distinct from shared documents
avoid_for: a shared/team document with no single owner emphasis
possible_misreadings: could be read simply as 'shared document with a collaborator' rather than 'personal/owned document' without context

name: team-owner
denotation: a shield outline containing a solid five-pointed star
best_for: representing a team-owner or lead role badge
a special/elevated status indicator distinct from a plain admin shield
avoid_for: a generic security/protection concept unrelated to roles (the star specifically implies rank or favorite status, not just protection)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with the plain admin shield icon (person-in-shield) if not viewed closely — the star vs. person silhouette is the distinguishing detail

name: user-unlock
denotation: a person silhouette with a checkmark badge attached at the lower right
connotations: granted permission tied to an individual
best_for: indicating a user's access has been approved/verified
an authorization-confirmation action on a specific person
avoid_for: a pending or unverified state (the checkmark specifically implies confirmation)
possible_misreadings: the checkmark badge visually resembles 'invite-user's plus badge at a glance — distinguished by check vs. plus shape, meaning 'verified' rather than 'added'

name: plugins
denotation: an electrical plug shape with two prongs and a rounded head
avoid_for: literal electrical/power settings unrelated to software integrations
possible_misreadings: in a non-software context could be read literally as an electrical power icon

name: data-flow
denotation: a rounded diamond outline containing a horizontal arrow with a branch splitting off
connotations: data moving and splitting between systems
best_for: representing data pipelines or data flow between systems
avoid_for: a single linear one-directional process with no branching (a plain arrow would suffice)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'redirect' or 'export' icon if the diamond frame is overlooked

name: dataprocessing
denotation: a rounded diamond outline containing a fast-forward double chevron
connotations: moving quickly through a pipeline step
best_for: representing an active data-processing or pipeline-execution step
a 'run/process' action button in a workflow
avoid_for: a paused or idle processing state (the forward chevrons imply active motion)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'skip forward' media-player control outside a data context

name: processItem
denotation: a rounded diamond outline containing a single forward-pointing chevron
best_for: representing an individual step/node within a larger process or pipeline
a process-item entity icon in a workflow diagram
avoid_for: representing the whole multi-step pipeline at once (this denotes one item/step, see dataprocessing/data-flow for whole-pipeline icons)
possible_misreadings: visually close to 'dataprocessing' (single vs. double chevron) — easy to confuse the two if viewed briefly

name: dataproduct-ai
denotation: a 3D open box/cube outline with a sparkle/star burst at its top-right corner
visual_elements: cube outline with visible top and front panel lines
connotations: something special or generated by AI
best_for: labeling an AI-powered or AI-enhanced data product
highlighting a new/smart feature with a sparkle cue
avoid_for: a plain, non-AI data product (use the sparkle-free dataproduct icon for that)
possible_misreadings: the sparkle could be read generically as 'new/featured' rather than specifically 'AI' without label context

name: dataproduct
denotation: a 3D open box/cube outline with visible top seam lines
best_for: representing a data product entity in a catalog
avoid_for: an AI-specific or enhanced product distinction (use dataproduct-ai for that nuance)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic shipping/inventory box icon outside a data-product context

name: marketplace
denotation: a storefront/market stall shape with a scalloped awning and a rectangular counter
connotations: a place to browse and acquire offerings
best_for: representing a marketplace or storefront section for browsing data products
avoid_for: a single product detail view (this represents the collective marketplace/venue, not one item)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a literal physical market/shop icon outside a software/data-product context

name: radiobutton-indeterminate
denotation: a filled circle with a horizontal dash/minus bar across its center
visual_elements: negative-space bar cut through the fill
best_for: indicating a partially-selected group of items
bulk-select UI showing 'some but not all' selected
avoid_for: fully selected/confirmed states (use a filled dot instead)
binary yes/no toggles with no partial state
possible_misreadings: could read as a 'do not enter' or 'remove' icon due to the minus bar
could be mistaken for a mute/no-entry symbol

name: radiobutton-selected
denotation: a solid ring/donut shape with a filled dot centered inside it
connotations: commitment to one option among several
best_for: depicting a selected/active option in a single-choice list
avoid_for: multi-select contexts (a checkbox icon reads more accurately)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a target/bullseye simplification
could be confused with a record/on-air button

name: radiobutton-unselected
denotation: an empty circular ring/outline with no fill inside
best_for: depicting an unselected/available option in a single-choice list
avoid_for: representing a complete process or a full loop (too plain/ambiguous)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a letter O or a zero
could read as a generic 'bullet point' circle

name: shrink-all
denotation: four arrows in the corners of a square all pointing inward toward the center
best_for: collapsing an expanded panel or fullscreen view back to normal size
compressing a diagram or map view
avoid_for: expanding or maximizing content (arrows point the wrong direction)
possible_misreadings: could be confused with 'expand-all' if arrow direction is misread at small sizes
could resemble a merge/converge diagram icon

name: in-revision
denotation: a circular refresh/redo arrow forming a partial loop with an arrowhead
visual_elements: open circular gap in the loop
best_for: status badge showing an item is currently being revised or updated
indicating an ongoing edit or reprocessing step
avoid_for: a fully completed or finalized state
a one-time action button for 'undo' specifically
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'refresh/reload' button rather than a status
could be confused with 'sync in progress'

name: in-validation
denotation: a clock face with hour and minute hands showing a specific time
visual_elements: circular clock outline as dark badge
two clock hands meeting at center
hands pointing to roughly 10 and 8 o'clock positions
connotations: something is under scrutiny before being finalized
best_for: status badge showing an item is pending validation or sign-off
indicating a time-sensitive review stage in a workflow
avoid_for: representing an alarm or scheduled reminder specifically
showing elapsed/historical time rather than a pending state
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'time' or 'schedule' icon unrelated to validation
could be mistaken for a duration/history icon

name: proposed
denotation: a question mark inside a solid circular badge
best_for: status badge showing an item is a proposal awaiting decision
avoid_for: a help/FAQ button (context here is status, not assistance)
possible_misreadings: could be read as a generic 'help' icon rather than a workflow status
could be confused with an error or unknown-value indicator

name: initiative-objective
denotation: a hexagonal shield-like outline with a dart/pin striking a bullseye-like dot inside it
connotations: precision targeting within a broader initiative
best_for: representing a specific measurable objective tied to a larger initiative
avoid_for: representing the initiative container itself without a specific objective
generic security/shield contexts (shape reads as strategy, not protection)
possible_misreadings: the hexagon outline could be mistaken for a generic badge or security shield
could be confused with a 'settings/config' icon due to hexagon shape alone

name: initiative
denotation: a plain hexagon outline with no interior markings
connotations: a structured unit or building block
best_for: representing a strategic initiative or program as an organizational unit
generic node in a strategy hierarchy diagram

a library or stack of favorites rather than a single one
best_for: a saved-items library or list view containing multiple bookmarks
avoid_for: a single save action (use singular bookmark icon for one item)
possible_misreadings: the overlap could read as 'duplicate' or 'copy' rather than a plural collection

name: border_all
denotation: a 2x2 grid of four filled squares with visible gaps forming a cross of borders
visual_elements: four equal solid squares arranged in a grid
thin gaps between them forming a plus-shaped border pattern
connotations: a fully bordered table or grid cell
best_for: a toolbar button that applies borders to every edge of a table/cell selection
avoid_for: partial border operations (there are separate icons for top/bottom/left/right only)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 4-app grid/menu launcher icon

name: border_bottom
denotation: a dot-grid table with a solid line only along the bottom edge
visual_elements: 3x3 array of small dots representing cell corners
single bold horizontal bar at the bottom
connotations: applying emphasis or a rule specifically to the bottom edge of a table/section
best_for: a toolbar control to add/toggle a border on just the bottom edge of a selected table region
possible_misreadings: dot grid alone (without noting which edge is bold) could be confused with other border_* variants

name: border_clear
denotation: a dot-grid pattern with no solid border lines at all
visual_elements: evenly spaced grid of small dots only, no bars or outline
best_for: a toolbar action to strip all borders from a table or cell selection
avoid_for: indicating an actively bordered/framed state (this is specifically the 'no border' variant)
possible_misreadings: dot grid could be read as a data/scatter icon rather than 'no border', since there's no border cue at all beyond absence

name: border_color
denotation: a diagonal pencil with a bold horizontal line beneath it
visual_elements: pencil shape tilted diagonally with pointed tip
solid horizontal bar underneath representing a drawn line
connotations: choosing or applying a color/style to a drawn border or line
best_for: a control for setting the color of table/cell borders or drawn lines
avoid_for: generic text editing (the underline bar ties this specifically to a border/line, not free text)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'edit'/'compose' pencil icon if the underline bar isn't distinguished from a text baseline

name: border_horizontal
denotation: a dot-grid table with a solid horizontal bar cutting through the middle row
visual_elements: grid of dots above and below
bold horizontal line splitting the grid at the middle
best_for: adding a horizontal rule between rows in a table
avoid_for: vertical dividers or full outer borders
possible_misreadings: easily confused with border_bottom/border_top if the middle position of the bar isn't noticed

name: border_inner
denotation: a dot-grid table with a bold plus-shaped cross through its center
visual_elements: grid of dots in the four quadrants
bold vertical and horizontal lines crossing in the middle
connotations: adding only the internal dividing lines of a table, not the outer frame
best_for: applying only interior gridlines to a table while leaving the outer edge unbordered
avoid_for: outer-only border operations (this is the inverse: interior lines only)
possible_misreadings: the plus-cross shape could be mistaken for a 'center' alignment or targeting/crosshair icon out of context

name: border_left
denotation: a dot-grid table with a solid vertical bar down the left edge
visual_elements: single bold vertical bar along the left side
connotations: emphasizing or framing the left edge of a table/section
best_for: a toolbar control to add a border to just the left edge of a table selection
avoid_for: right, top, or bottom-only border operations
possible_misreadings: mirror-image confusion with border_right if orientation isn't checked carefully

name: border_outer
denotation: a dot-grid table with a bold L-shaped line tracing the top and left edges
visual_elements: thick corner line forming an L along the top-left perimeter
connotations: defining the outer boundary/frame of a table, partially shown
best_for: applying/toggling the outer frame border around a table or selection
possible_misreadings: the partial L-corner rendering could be mistaken for a 'crop' or 'resize corner' icon

name: border_right
denotation: a dot-grid table with a solid vertical bar down the right edge
visual_elements: single bold vertical bar along the right side
connotations: emphasizing or framing the right edge of a table/section
best_for: a toolbar control to add a border to just the right edge of a table selection
avoid_for: left, top, or bottom-only border operations
possible_misreadings: mirror-image confusion with border_left if orientation isn't checked carefully

name: border_style
denotation: a dot-grid table with a bold horizontal bar along the top edge
visual_elements: thick horizontal bar across the top
connotations: choosing or previewing a line/border style for a table edge
best_for: a menu entry for choosing border line style/weight in a table editor
avoid_for: distinguishing specifically from border_top, which looks nearly identical — use context/label, not this icon alone, when the two must be told apart
possible_misreadings: visually almost indistinguishable from border_top; risks being swapped with it

name: border_top
denotation: a dot-grid table with a bold horizontal bar along the top edge
visual_elements: thick horizontal bar across the top
connotations: emphasizing or framing the top edge of a table/section
best_for: a toolbar control to add a border to just the top edge of a table selection
avoid_for: bottom, left, or right-only border operations
possible_misreadings: nearly identical to border_style; also could be confused with border_bottom if flipped

name: border_vertical
denotation: a dot-grid table split by a bold vertical bar through the middle column
visual_elements: dots to the left and right
bold vertical line dividing the grid at center
best_for: adding a vertical rule between columns in a table
avoid_for: horizontal dividers or full outer borders
possible_misreadings: could be confused with border_left/border_right if the centered position of the bar is missed

name: boy
denotation: a simplified human figure with a round head and rectangular torso/legs, no dress/skirt shape
visual_elements: torso rendered as a single trapezoid-like body block
connotations: a male child or young person
a generic individual user represented simply
best_for: representing a child/male demographic, family or parental-control content, or a generic person avatar
avoid_for: gender-neutral persona slots (this glyph specifically reads as a boy in the Material set, distinct from the plain 'person' icon)
possible_misreadings: at small size can be nearly indistinguishable from the generic 'person'/'man' icon

name: branding_watermark
denotation: a rounded rectangle frame (like a screen or card) with a small solid rectangle stamped in the lower-right corner
visual_elements: small filled rectangle badge positioned bottom-right inside the frame
connotations: a logo or brand mark overlaid on content
ownership or attribution stamped onto media
best_for: watermarking, branding overlays on video/images, or attribution/copyright marking features
avoid_for: generic image or screen icons unrelated to branding (the corner badge is the specific branding cue)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a 'picture-in-picture' or generic screen/monitor icon if the corner badge isn't read as a brand mark

name: breakfast_dining
denotation: a slice of toast/bread with a diamond shape in the center
visual_elements: diamond (rotated square) cutout or motif in the middle
connotations: breakfast food or a morning meal
casual dining or a cafe menu item
best_for: a food-delivery or restaurant app's breakfast category
possible_misreadings: the diamond motif could read as a card-suit symbol or jewel if the toast outline isn't clearly perceived

name: brightness_1
denotation: a plain solid-outline circle
visual_elements: single ring/circle outline with no interior marks
connotations: a full, unmarked disc — a neutral status dot or a placeholder shape
best_for: a maximum-brightness setting option, a neutral status dot, or a simple circular bullet/marker
avoid_for: conveying any specific brightness LEVEL beyond 'full/plain' since it carries no shading cue at all
possible_misreadings: being a plain circle, it's highly ambiguous alone and easily read as 'record button', 'radio button', or just a decorative dot rather than brightness-specific

name: brightness_2
denotation: a crescent moon shape formed by two overlapping circle arcs
visual_elements: inner concave arc carving out the crescent shape
connotations: nighttime, dim lighting, or a sleep/dark mode state
best_for: a night-mode/dark-mode toggle or a low-brightness display setting
possible_misreadings: a generic 'moon' icon could be confused with weather (clear night) rather than a brightness/display control

name: brightness_3
denotation: a slimmer crescent moon shape than brightness_2, formed by two overlapping arcs
visual_elements: deeper inner arc producing a narrower sliver moon
connotations: a dimmer or more minimal night/dark state than its sibling icon
best_for: a lower brightness-level step in a settings sequence, or a night-mode icon
avoid_for: daytime/bright contexts; also avoid using interchangeably with brightness_2 if a specific intensity step matters
possible_misreadings: nearly identical to brightness_2 — the two are easily swapped without careful comparison

name: brightness_4
denotation: an eight-pointed star/sun-burst outline containing a crescent moon
connotations: a mixed day/night or auto-brightness state
best_for: an 'automatic' or 'adaptive' brightness mode toggle combining day/night sensing
avoid_for: a pure night-only or pure day-only setting (the combined star+moon implies a hybrid/auto state)
possible_misreadings: the star-burst frame could be mistaken for an achievement badge or sparkle/rating icon if the moon inside isn't noticed

name: brightness_5
denotation: an eight-pointed star/sun-burst outline with the left half filled solid
visual_elements: vertical split with left half solid black, right half empty
connotations: a midpoint setting between low and high
best_for: indicating a mid-level brightness setting in a stepped control
avoid_for: full or auto brightness states (use brightness_1/brightness_7-style full-sun icons for those)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a 'contrast' icon (half-filled circle) rather than specifically brightness, since half-fill is also the classic contrast/theme-toggle glyph

name: brightness_6
denotation: an eight-pointed star/sun-burst outline with an unfilled circle centered inside
visual_elements: plain open circle in the middle
connotations: a standard sun/brightness icon in a neutral, non-active or default state
best_for: a standard brightness control icon, light-mode toggle, or sunny-weather indicator
avoid_for: a filled/high-intensity sun state where a solid center might better communicate 'full brightness'
possible_misreadings: easily confused with other brightness_N variants (4, 5, 7) that share the same star frame with different center fills

name: brightness_7
denotation: an eight-pointed scalloped badge outline with a solid filled circle centered inside
connotations: full intensity, everything switched fully on
best_for: indicating a setting at its maximum or fully-on state
avoid_for: anything about time of day (no sun rays or sky context, just an abstract badge)
certification claims requiring a ribbon or star, which this is not
possible_misreadings: could read as an award/certified seal rather than a brightness control since the badge shape resembles a rosette
could be mistaken for a compass or gear at small sizes

name: brightness_auto
denotation: the same scalloped badge outline containing a capital letter A instead of a filled shape
connotations: a system making a decision instead of a person
the same badge family signaling this is a brightness-related variant
best_for: explaining a system that self-adjusts a setting without user input
avoid_for: anything unrelated to an on/off or leveled setting since the 'A' strongly implies automation specifically
possible_misreadings: the letter A alone could be read as a grade, an app initial, or an anchor letter unrelated to automation if the badge shape isn't recognized

name: brightness_high
denotation: the scalloped badge outline with a hollow/outlined ring centered inside
connotations: a high but not saturated-solid level, an intermediate-to-high reading
openness or partial state compared to the fully solid variant
best_for: a 'high brightness' step in a settings slider or menu
distinguishing tiers of a light/display setting from other badge variants
avoid_for: representing an empty or off state, since the ring still implies presence of a value
possible_misreadings: easy to confuse with brightness_medium or brightness_low at small scale since the differentiator is only the ring's fill/size

name: brightness_low
denotation: the scalloped badge outline with a small dot centered inside
connotations: a minimal or dim level of a setting
best_for: a 'low brightness' step in a settings menu or slider
showing the minimum end of a level scale
avoid_for: representing complete darkness/off, since a dot is still visibly present
possible_misreadings: at very small render sizes the center dot can nearly disappear, reading as an empty badge (looking like an 'off' state)

name: brightness_medium
denotation: the scalloped badge outline with a medium-sized filled circle centered inside
best_for: a 'medium brightness' step in a level scale between low and high/full variants
avoid_for: conveying a precise numeric value, since the size difference from other variants is subtle
possible_misreadings: can be visually confused with brightness_7 (full) or brightness_high if not shown adjacent for size comparison

name: broadcast_on_home
denotation: a smartphone/remote-shaped rectangle with a curved bracket above it, next to a separate circular dot emitting two concentric signal arcs
visual_elements: curved handle/bracket line above the device
connotations: a home device broadcasting or receiving a wireless signal
best_for: a device actively transmitting/receiving a local wireless signal
avoid_for: public/mass broadcast contexts (radio, TV) since 'home' in the name is depicted via a small device, not a house shape here
possible_misreadings: the device shape plus arcs could be mistaken for a doorbell or a remote-control icon rather than 'broadcasting'

name: broadcast_on_personal
denotation: a house outline with a small figure/silhouette-like dot inside it, and a signal-tower icon with concentric arcs positioned at the house's lower right
visual_elements: small circular dot inside the house
connotations: personal or in-home presence being broadcast/detected
best_for: personal hotspot or presence-broadcasting features tied to a home/household context
avoid_for: generic house/real-estate icons, since the antenna and arcs add a signal-specific meaning that would be misleading there
possible_misreadings: could be read as a home security/alarm icon due to the house-plus-signal combination rather than personal broadcasting specifically

name: broken_image
denotation: a square picture-frame outline containing a jagged zigzag line instead of a normal mountain/landscape glyph
visual_elements: jagged zigzag line resembling a crack or broken mountain shape
connotations: failure to load or display media
corruption or damage to visual content
an error state presented as a distorted version of a normal picture icon
best_for: a placeholder shown when an image fails to load or is missing
flagging corrupted or unavailable media assets
avoid_for: a normal/working image placeholder (that role belongs to the plain picture-frame icon with a mountain, not this jagged version)
possible_misreadings: the zigzag alone, out of context, could read as a stock/finance line chart rather than 'broken' image

name: browse_gallery
denotation: a circular clock face with an hour and minute hand, flanked by a right-facing bracket/parenthesis-like curve on the right side
visual_elements: clock hands pointing to a time
curved bracket line beside the circle
connotations: scrolling or paging forward through time-ordered content
a media timeline or history view
best_for: a UI control for paging through a chronologically-ordered photo or media gallery
avoid_for: a plain clock/time-telling function, since the added bracket implies motion/browsing beyond just showing time
possible_misreadings: without the bracket cue, could be mistaken for a plain clock or alarm icon; could also suggest 'fast-forward through history' in a video player

name: browser_not_supported
denotation: a rounded rectangle browser-window shape with a diagonal line struck through it
visual_elements: diagonal slash line corner to corner
best_for: messaging that a browser or web feature is unsupported or blocked
avoid_for: a generic 'close window' or 'delete' action, since the slash specifically implies unsupported/forbidden rather than removal
possible_misreadings: the slashed-rectangle pattern is a generic 'not allowed' symbol and could be mistaken for 'no photos allowed' or 'do not enter' if the rectangle isn't read as a browser chrome

name: browser_updated
denotation: a monitor/screen outline with a downward-pointing arrow entering a bracket shape above it
visual_elements: open bracket/tray shape the arrow points into
connotations: downloading or installing a new version
refreshing software to the latest state
an update being applied to a device
best_for: an update-available or update-in-progress notification for software/browsers
avoid_for: general file downloads unrelated to software/version updates, where a plainer download-tray icon fits better
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic 'download to desktop' icon if the update-specific context/label is absent

name: brunch_dining
denotation: an oval serving plate/platter next to an upright cocktail glass with a stem and swizzle stick
visual_elements: thin stick or garnish protruding from the glass
connotations: a leisurely meal paired with a drink
best_for: restaurant, cafe, or event menus involving food service with drinks
avoid_for: fine dining or fast food specifically, since the cocktail glass cue skews toward brunch/bar-style service
possible_misreadings: could be read generically as 'bar' or 'cocktails' if the plate isn't noticed, losing the dining half of the meaning

name: brush
denotation: a paintbrush drawn at a diagonal, with a pointed dripping tip at the bottom and a rounded ferrule/handle at top
visual_elements: a drip or swirl detail at the brush point
avoid_for: writing/text-editing actions, which are better served by a pen or pencil icon
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a makeup/cosmetics brush or a cleaning brush depending on context, since the drip could also look like bristle spread

name: bubble_chart
denotation: three circles of different sizes arranged loosely together — one large outlined ring, one medium ring, and one small filled dot
connotations: clusters or groupings of varying magnitude
data points of different weights or values
a scatter of related but distinct items
best_for: labeling a bubble-chart view in analytics or BI tools
representing multi-dimensional or weighted data comparisons
avoid_for: simple bar/line chart contexts, since circles specifically imply the bubble-chart chart type, not general 'chart' functionality
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a Venn-diagram or 'group/community' icon since it's just circles without axes or a clear chart frame

name: bug_report
denotation: a stylized insect/bug body viewed from above, with a segmented oval torso, antennae-like curved lines on top, and legs extending to both sides
visual_elements: oval segmented body with two horizontal dashes
leg lines on left and right sides
connotations: an infestation or hidden problem to be found and fixed
best_for: issue-tracking and bug-reporting features in developer tools
avoid_for: literal entomology or nature content, where the stylization would read as too abstract/technical
possible_misreadings: at a glance the segmented body could be mistaken for a battery or a barcode-like object rather than an insect if the legs/antennae aren't noticed

name: build
denotation: a wrench drawn diagonally across the icon, with a hexagonal open jaw at one end and a rounded handle at the other
avoid_for: software construction/compilation specifically without a settings connotation, since a wrench reads more as 'fix/configure' than 'compile'
possible_misreadings: a bare wrench is one of the most overloaded UI icons — could mean settings, repair, tools, or 'under construction' depending purely on context

name: build_circle
denotation: the same diagonal wrench shape as the plain build icon, but enclosed within a circular outline border
visual_elements: diagonal wrench with hexagonal jaw and rounded handle inside the ring
connotations: a contained or scoped maintenance action, framed as a discrete button/badge rather than a loose glyph
a service or repair status indicator
best_for: a clickable settings/maintenance button where a circular badge frame is the visual convention
status indicators showing something is in a serviceable/configurable state
avoid_for: inline text icons where a plain (non-circled) wrench is more compact and appropriate
possible_misreadings: same overloaded meaning as the plain wrench (settings vs. repair vs. tools) — the circle only adds 'framed/actionable', not semantic specificity

name: bungalow
denotation: a single-story house shape with a triangular roof and a circular window/vent in the gable
visual_elements: small circle in the gable/peak area
best_for: real estate listings for single-story homes
avoid_for: multi-story buildings, apartments, or commercial property, where a taller building icon fits better
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'home' icon (house + dot) since the round window detail is subtle at small sizes

name: burst_mode
denotation: two overlapping camera-shutter-like ellipse shapes stacked with a small gap, layered to suggest depth/repetition
visual_elements: vertical gap indicating separation between the two layers
connotations: rapid, repeated capture in quick succession
continuous shooting rather than a single moment
best_for: a camera app's burst/continuous-shooting mode toggle
avoid_for: a single-photo capture action, which needs a plain camera icon instead
possible_misreadings: the stacked-oval look could be mistaken for a stack of cards, plates, or a battery-level indicator without camera context

name: bus_alert
denotation: a front-on bus body with two circular wheels underneath and an exclamation mark inside a circle at the upper right
connotations: a transit disruption or service alert
a warning tied specifically to a bus route or vehicle
best_for: transit apps signaling a delay, reroute, or service issue on a bus line
avoid_for: general transportation icons without an alert/warning context, where a plain bus icon (no exclamation) is correct instead
possible_misreadings: could be misread as 'new bus available' or 'bus stop nearby' if the exclamation badge isn't parsed as a warning specifically

name: business
denotation: a tall rectangular building facade made of a grid of small square windows
visual_elements: grid of small filled and outlined squares representing windows
best_for: representing a company, organization, or corporate/commercial property in a directory or map pin
avoid_for: residential housing contexts, where a house-shaped icon is the correct choice instead
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'apartment building' or 'hotel' icon since window-grid buildings look similar across those categories

name: business_center
denotation: a briefcase with a carrying handle on top and a small square latch/lock detail centered on the front panel
visual_elements: small square latch in the center
best_for: business-travel amenities (hotel 'business center'), professional/corporate service categories, career-related sections
avoid_for: casual personal-item contexts like luggage for leisure travel, where a suitcase icon fits better
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a toolbox or first-aid kit if the handle-and-latch proportions are rendered differently at small sizes

name: cabin
denotation: a peaked-roof house/cabin shape with three horizontal lines across the body suggesting log or plank construction, and a small curl of smoke above the roof
visual_elements: horizontal banding lines on the house body
small curved smoke wisp above the peak
connotations: a cozy retreat away from urban life
best_for: vacation rental listings, outdoor/nature travel categories, rustic lodging options
avoid_for: urban housing or modern apartment contexts, where the log-plank detail and smoke would be misleading
possible_misreadings: the smoke curl could be mistaken for a decorative flourish or bird if not viewed at sufficient resolution

name: cable
denotation: a wavy cable line connecting two plug/connector heads, each capped with a small rectangular connector shape
visual_elements: two rectangular connector plugs at each end
small notch details on each connector
connotations: wired/hardware linkage as opposed to wireless
a literal data or power cable
best_for: representing a physical/wired cable accessory or a wired-connection setting
avoid_for: wireless connectivity features, which need signal-wave iconography instead of a literal cable
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a chain link or DNA-strand icon if the connector-plug ends aren't clearly rendered

name: cached
denotation: two curved arrows forming an incomplete circular loop, one arrowhead pointing up-left and the other pointing down-right
connotations: renewal or updating from a stored/cached state
best_for: a refresh or sync button in an app
avoid_for: a literal recycling/environmental context, where a different arrow arrangement (three arrows) is the conventional recycling symbol
possible_misreadings: nearly identical to generic 'refresh' or 'loop/repeat' icons — without a label, viewers can't distinguish 'cache refresh' from plain 'reload' or 'repeat playback'

name: cake
denotation: a layered cake shape with a single lit candle on top, drawn as two stacked rectangular tiers
visual_elements: candle with a flame droplet shape on top
connotations: a milestone or anniversary being marked
best_for: birthday reminders or celebratory notifications in calendar/social apps
avoid_for: general food/dining categories where a birthday-specific connotation would be misleading
possible_misreadings: at small sizes the candle flame could be mistaken for a small flag or pin marker on top of a generic rectangular object

name: calculate
denotation: a rectangular calculator body with a minus and X symbol on the top row and a plus and equals symbol on the bottom row
visual_elements: horizontal divider line separating display from buttons
avoid_for: data-entry or spreadsheet-grid contexts without a computation angle, where a table/grid icon is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a generic keypad or remote control if the specific operator symbols aren't legible at small size

name: calendar_month
denotation: a rectangular calendar page with two tabs on top and a 2-column by 3-row grid of small dots representing dates
connotations: a monthly overview of scheduled dates
best_for: a monthly calendar view toggle in a scheduling app
avoid_for: a single-date/today view, which is better represented by calendar_today's blank-page variant
possible_misreadings: the dot grid could be mistaken for a keypad or a QR-code-like pattern rather than dates if rendered too small to read as a calendar page

name: calendar_today
denotation: a rectangular calendar page with two binding tabs on top and an otherwise blank body below a header band
connotations: a specific single day or the current date
a blank slate ready to be filled with an event
best_for: a 'jump to today' button or a generic date/calendar reference where no specific grid detail is needed
avoid_for: a full monthly grid view, where calendar_month's dotted-grid variant communicates that more explicitly
possible_misreadings: without added detail, could be read as a generic 'note' or 'document' icon rather than specifically calendar-related, since the body is blank

name: calendar_view_day
denotation: a horizontal rectangular outline with a single thick horizontal bar centered inside it, plus a thin line above and below
visual_elements: thin line segment above the bar
thin line segment below the bar
connotations: focusing on a single day/row within a larger sequence
one item emphasized among a stacked list
best_for: a 'day view' toggle switch within a calendar app's view-mode selector
avoid_for: week or month view contexts, which need multiple equally-weighted bars instead of one emphasized bar
possible_misreadings: without the calendar-view family context, could look like a generic 'featured item' or 'highlight row' icon unrelated to dates

name: calendar_view_month
denotation: a rounded rectangle frame subdivided into a 3x3 grid of equal square cells
connotations: a full month laid out as a grid of days
best_for: a 'month view' toggle in a calendar app's view-mode selector
avoid_for: day or week view contexts, and generic non-calendar grid/table layouts, where a plain grid icon without calendar framing is more accurate
possible_misreadings: could be confused with a generic 'grid layout' or 'apps launcher' icon (like a 3x3 app-grid) outside a calendar-view context

name: calendar_view_week
denotation: a rounded rectangle frame divided into four equal vertical column strips
connotations: a week laid out as parallel day-columns
a mid-level scheduling granularity between single-day and full-month
best_for: a 'week view' toggle in a calendar app's view-mode selector
avoid_for: month or day view contexts; also a poor fit for generic column-layout UI describing content unrelated to dates
possible_misreadings: the vertical-stripe pattern alone could be mistaken for a generic 'columns layout' or 'blinds' icon without the calendar-view family context

name: call
denotation: a stylized handset silhouette shown as two rounded trapezoid ear/mouth pieces joined by a diagonal curved body
connotations: making or receiving a phone call
best_for: a call/dial button in a phone or contacts app
avoid_for: messaging or video-call features, which need distinct chat-bubble or camera iconography instead
possible_misreadings: is one of the most universally recognized icons, so misreading is rare, but at extreme rotation could be mistaken for an abstract ribbon shape

name: call_end
denotation: the same curved handset-arc shape as the call icon but rotated so it forms a downward-opening arc, with the two end-pieces angled outward like a hung-up receiver
connotations: terminating or hanging up a call
a stop/decline action tied specifically to telephony
best_for: the hang-up/decline button in a call interface
avoid_for: a general 'stop' or 'cancel' action outside of telephony, where the handset-arc shape would be an unclear metaphor
possible_misreadings: out of context (no red color, no phone UI) the arc shape alone could be mistaken for a bridge, rainbow, or generic curve rather than 'hang up'

name: call_made
denotation: a straight diagonal arrow pointing to the upper right, originating from a lower-left point
connotations: movement away from the current context toward something external
best_for: marking an outgoing call in a call-log list
showing an upward/positive trend in a metric
avoid_for: incoming actions, which need the mirrored down-left arrow (call_received) instead
possible_misreadings: highly overloaded: identical arrow shape is reused across telephony, external-link, and financial-trend contexts, so meaning depends entirely on surrounding UI

name: call_merge
denotation: two diagonal lines merging into one upward arrow
a fork shape flipped upside down forming a Y into an arrowhead
connotations: convergence of separate paths into one
best_for: merging two data sources or branches into one
consolidation steps in a process diagram
avoid_for: splitting or diverging concepts (this reads as convergence, not division)
possible_misreadings: could be mistaken for a simple upward arrow with a stray mark if the merging line is not noticed

name: call_missed
denotation: a bent arrow pointing to upper-left like a hook or check-mark reversed
best_for: indicating a missed or incoming failed communication
flagging an unanswered request or dropped interaction
possible_misreadings: easily confused with call_missed_outgoing or call_received since all three share the same bent-arrow motif with different orientations

name: call_missed_outgoing
denotation: a diagonal arrow starting horizontal then bending to point down-right toward an arrowhead
connotations: an outgoing attempt that wasn't completed
a redirected or diverted outbound action
best_for: outbound call or request that did not connect
tracking failed outreach attempts in CRM contexts
possible_misreadings: visually close to call_split or call_merge; the specific elbow direction is the only distinguishing cue

name: call_received
denotation: a straight diagonal arrow pointing down-left into an open bracket/corner shape
visual_elements: L-shaped corner bracket at the base
best_for: representing successful inbound communication or data receipt

name: connected_tv
